risks: Consumption plan (Y1) introduces cold starts (1-3s) for webhook-driven workload; Azure best practices now recommend Flex Consumption (FC1) over Y1 Dynamic
Cosmos DB has publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled with no firewall rules or private endpoint, exposing the data plane to the public internet
Single-region deployment (eastus) with LRS storage and no geo-redundancy provides no disaster recovery capability
Storage account uses connection-string-based AzureWebJobsStorage instead of identity-based access, which conflicts with the plan's own managed-identity-first principle
No RBAC role assignments are defined for the managed identity to access Key Vault, Cosmos DB, or Storage — the identity exists but has no permissions
Storage account does not explicitly disable local auth (allowSharedKeyAccess not set to false), leaving key-based access available
correctionsRecommended: Upgrade App Service Plan from Y1 (Consumption) to FC1 (Flex Consumption) with functionAppConfig and deployment.storage configuration per Azure Functions deployment best practices

Add RBAC role assignment resources for the managed identity: Storage Blob Data Owner, Storage Blob Data Contributor, Storage Queue Data Contributor, Storage Table Data Contributor, and Monitoring Metrics Publisher on the storage account
Add RBAC role assignment for Key Vault Secrets User on the Key Vault for the managed identity
Add RBAC role assignment for Cosmos DB data-plane access (e.g., Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor) for the managed identity
Set allowSharedKeyAccess to false on the storage account and switch AzureWebJobsStorage to identity-based connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName)
Add Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings resources for Function App, Key Vault, and Cosmos DB to send logs to the Log Analytics workspace
Restrict Cosmos DB publicNetworkAccess or add IP/VNet firewall rules
Add a resource tagging strategy (e.g., environment, project, cost-center) for operational governance
